title: digitalis × purpurascens subsp. lutescens
intro: Digitalis × purpurascens subsp. lutescens, also known as a hybrid foxglove, is a subspecies of the foxglove plant (Digitalis purpurea) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the cardiac glycosides it contains, which have been used to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis × purpurascens subsp. lutescens include its ability to strengthen heart muscle contractions and improve cardiac output, making it a potential treatment for various heart conditions. Herbal preparations of Digitalis × purpurascens subsp. lutescens typically involve extracting the cardiac glycosides from the leaves, which can be processed into tinctures, capsules, or teas for medicinal use.
